issues.problem: Although built on BaseDialog (which sets fullScreen={useIsMobile()}), this dialog passes its own slotProps={{paper:{sx:{... maxWidth:560, height:'85vh', borderRadius:2, overflow:'hidden'}}}}. The explicit height:'85vh' and maxWidth:560 also win over MUI's fullScreen paper styles. Net result: on a 360px phone the import dialog is NOT full-screen — it floats as an 85vh card with rounded corners and side margins, defeating the shipped full-screen backbone.
issues.fix: Make the paper override responsive: spread BaseDialog's fullscreen behaviour. Either (a) gate the override with useIsMobile() — on mobile drop maxWidth/height/borderRadius and use { height:'100%', borderRadius:0, border:'none' }; or (b) move height:'85vh' into contentSx/an inner Box and let BaseDialog own the paper sx so its full-screen branch survives. issues.location: lines 85-95 inner Box `maxWidth: width || '100%'` where `width` comes from the IMAGE entity data
issues.problem: If an embedded image entity stored an explicit numeric `width` (e.g. 600), the inner container's maxWidth becomes 600px. It's still bounded by the parent `width:'100%'` and the `` is `maxWidth:'100%'`, so it shouldn't truly overflow 360px — but the `maxWidth: width` is a latent risk if `width` is ever applied as a hard px on the img or a future layout. Loading placeholder also sets `minWidth: 200` (fits 328, OK).
issues.fix: Clamp it: `maxWidth: width ? \`min(${typeof width==='number'?width+'px':width}, 100%)\` : '100%'`. This guarantees a stored width never exceeds the column even if the constraint chain changes.

issues.problem: APP_HEADER_HEIGHT is hardcoded to 64 and used as APP_VIEWPORT_OFFSET to compute height: calc(100vh - 64px) on md+. On phones the AppHeader is 56px (HEADER_HEIGHT xs), so the constant is wrong for mobile — but more importantly the height calc is md-only here so the page height is 'auto' on xs, which is correct. The hardcoded 64 should instead import HEADER_HEIGHT/BELOW_HEADER_HEIGHT from styles/layout.ts so it tracks the 56/64 token split rather than duplicating a magic number.

issues.problem: The chat is a partial-height bottom sheet (fixed height:880px capped at maxHeight:'92vh', borderTopRadius 16) — NOT a full-screen takeover. The task explicitly requires the phone chat to be a full-screen takeover with the input bar sitting above the keyboard. At 360px the sheet width is xs:'100%' (good), but the 92vh cap leaves an 8vh dead strip of backdrop at the top, the rounded top corners read as a half-modal, and when the on-screen keyboard opens it overlaps the AIChatInterface input bar (the sheet height is viewport-relative and does not shrink for the keyboard / visualViewport). Add safe-area padding: paddingTop:'env(safe-area-inset-top)' on the header and paddingBottom:'env(safe-area-inset-bottom)' on the footer wrapper. Use 100dvh (dynamic viewport) so the sheet tracks the keyboard; the inner flex column already pins the input footer to the bottom. Keep the desktop bottom-sheet (sm+) as-is via the breakpoint object.
